frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: spatial representation chosen to maximize future expected reward

frameworks.name: predictive representation (successor representation)
frameworks.one_line: place cells encode expected future state occupancy, not pure geometry

frameworks.name: cognitive map
frameworks.one_line: purely geometric place-cell view that the paper argues is incomplete

frameworks.name: hierarchical planning via low-dimensional basis
frameworks.one_line: grid cells as eigenbasis enabling multiscale prediction

core_question: What kind of hippocampal spatial representation is most useful for maximizing future reward?
key_assumptions: place cell activity reflects a learned representation rather than fixed geometry
useful representations are those that support reward-directed behavior
entorhinal grid cells provide a basis set decomposition of hippocampal codes
manipulations: theoretical comparison of geometric vs predictive representations
dimensionality of the basis set encoding the predictive map
key_findings: place cell responses show predictive coding, reward sensitivity, and policy dependence beyond pure spatial coding
a predictive representation reproduces many non-geometric place cell properties
grid cells correspond to a low-dimensional basis set for the predictive representation
interpretations: the hippocampus functions as a predictive map shaped by reward-maximizing policies
grid cells support noise suppression and multiscale hierarchical planning via basis decomposition
